signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% YoY (Lower than 2.3-2.4% Consensus)** | NO | HIGH confidence[1][2][4]

South Korea's official CPI data, released Thursday by the Ministry of Data and Statistics, shows annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in February—below market forecasts of 2.3-2.4%—driven by oil prices but curbed by government fuel caps and food vouchers
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Solana Spot ETFs Record $5
signals.raw:
Solana Spot ETFs Record $5.04M Net Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**  
U.S. spot Solana ETFs saw $5.04M in net inflows on April 16, 2026, following $5.36M the prior day, with cumulative assets at $835M, signaling strong institutional demand.[3] This sustained capital inflow provides price support independent of retail trading, aligning with bullish technical setups like a descending wedge breakout
